caseTitle: Human Trafficking Sting in Steubenville Arrests 8 Men Seeking to Buy Sex
summary: A joint sting operation by the Mahoning Valley Human Trafficking Task Force and the Steubenville Police Department recently led to the arrest of eight men who allegedly attempted to buy sex, including one who sought sexual conduct with a minor.

caseTitle: Yost Warns Pornography Sites of Potential Lawsuits Over Violations of Age-Verification Law
summary: A review of 20 top pornography websites ordered by Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost revealed that only one is complying with Ohio’s recently enacted age-verification law.

caseTitle: IDENTIFIED: 1989 Franklin County John Doe
summary: In another victory for forensic genealogy, Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost and Franklin County Coroner Dr. Nate Overmire announced today that the skeletal remains of a white male found 36 years ago have been identified.

caseTitle: Yost Strikes Deal With Hebrew Union College to Protect Rare-Book Collection in Cincinnati
summary: A settlement brokered by Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost sets new ground rules for the management and removal of invaluable ancient artifacts housed in Cincinnati’s Klau Library at Hebrew Union College.

caseTitle: AG Yost’s Teen Ambassador Board Kicks Off Year With a Real-Time Experience
summary: Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost welcomed members of the 2025-26 Teen Ambassador Board this week by inviting them to sit in on a press conference announcing the results of a statewide human trafficking crackdown, Operation Next Door.

caseTitle: Attorney General Yost Honors Ohio Law Enforcement, Touts Best-in-Class Training at Annual Symposium
summary: Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost visited Ohio law enforcement officers at the 2025 Law Enforcement Training Symposium today to commend their courage, dedication and sacrifice and highlight the importance of first-rate training to prepare them for the ever-evolving challenges of the line of duty.

caseTitle: 135 Arrested in 'Operation Next Door' Crackdown on Human Trafficking Across Ohio
summary: A statewide human trafficking operation conducted last week by more than 100 law enforcement agencies resulted in the arrest of 135 people seeking to buy sex – including from minors, Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost announced today.

caseTitle: AG Yost Condemns Religious Violence in Wake of Michigan Shooting
summary: Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost issued the following statement regarding the attack on the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints chapel in Michigan:

caseTitle: AG Yost Sues Cincinnati Business Owners for Misrepresenting Refurbished Products as New
summary: Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost today announced a consumer protection lawsuit against a Cincinnati business and it owners for deceptively marketing refurbished Dyson products as new to consumers nationwide.

caseTitle: BCI Releases Age-Progression Image in Decades-Long Search for Missing Cleveland Man
summary: The Ohio Attorney General’s Bureau of Criminal Investigation hopes to spark new leads in the 2004 disappearance of a Cleveland man with the release of an image showing what he might look like today.

caseTitle: Former Caregiver at Retirement Community in Ashtabula County Sentenced to Prison for Rape
summary: A former caregiver at a Conneaut retirement community has been sentenced to 8-12 years in prison for raping elderly residents, Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost announced today.

caseTitle: AG Yost Sues Two Home Remodeling Contractors
summary: Lawsuits have been filed against two contractors who tried to fleece consumers, Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost announced today.

caseTitle: 16 Medicaid Providers Facing Fraud, Theft Charges
summary: Indictments filed this month by the office of Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost accuse 16 Medicaid providers of stealing a combined $1.7 million from the government health-care program for the needy.

caseTitle: Clark County Man Sentenced to Prison for 2023 Murder
summary: The killer of a 20-year-old Clark County man was sentenced this morning to 18 years to life in prison, Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost announced.

caseTitle: Bellevue Man Sentenced to 10 Years for Threatening to Kill Lawyers, Judge
summary: A Bellevue man who threatened to kill his lawyer, prosecutors, and the judge handling his criminal case has been sentenced to 10 years in prison, Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost announced today.

caseTitle: Columbus West Side Criminal Organization Indicted on Human Trafficking and Drug Charges
summary: Five members of a criminal organization accused of committing violent crimes and dealing narcotics to further a human trafficking enterprise were indicted by a Franklin County grand jury, Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost announced today.

caseTitle: AG Yost Appeals Ruling in Google Common Carrier Case
summary: Not ready to give up the fight for fairness in the marketplace, Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost today appealed a Delaware County Common Pleas judge’s recent decision in the Google “common carrier” case.

caseTitle: AG Yost Encourages High School Students to Apply for Teen Ambassador Board
summary: The office of Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost is accepting applications for Yost’s Teen Ambassador Board (TAB) for the 2025-26 academic year.

caseTitle: Washington County Man Convicted in 1995 Murders of Mother and Child
summary: Following a two-week trial, a Washington County jury found a Waterford man guilty of murdering a mother and her 17-month-old son three decades ago.

caseTitle: Yost Urges Appeals Court to Overturn Block on Parental-Consent Law
summary: A brief filed today with a federal appeals court lays out Ohio’s arguments for reversing a block on the state’s parental-consent law for social media.

caseTitle: 10 Medicaid Providers Facing Fraud, Theft Charges
summary: In indictments filed this week by the office of Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost, 10 Medicaid providers are accused of stealing a combined $1.9 million from the government health-care program for the needy.

caseTitle: City of Maumee Commits to Sewer Upgrades in Proposed Water Pollution Settlement
summary: The city of Maumee has agreed to make long-overdue upgrades to its sewer system to end decades of pollution in the Maumee River, Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost announced today.

caseTitle: Title and Summary Certified for Proposed ‘Equal Rights’ Constitutional Amendment
summary: The Ohio Attorney General’s Office today certified the petition title and summary for a proposed constitutional amendment titled “Ohio Equal Rights Amendment” which seeks to add a Section 23 to Article I of the Ohio Constitution.

caseTitle: Title and Summary Certified for Proposed ‘Right to Marry’ Constitutional Amendment
summary: The Ohio Attorney General’s Office today certified the petition title and summary for a proposed constitutional amendment titled “Ohio Right to Marry Amendment” which seeks to repeal Article XV, Section 11 of the Ohio Constitution.

caseTitle: Yost Seeks Full-Court Review, Reversal of Texas Border Decision
summary: Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost and South Carolina Attorney General Alan Wilson are leading a coalition of 22 states in urging a federal appeals court to reverse a recent U.S. District Court decision that blocked Texas’ right to enforce its state immigration law.

caseTitle: Human Trafficking Task Forces in Northeast Ohio Raid Massage Parlors Linked to Illegal Activity
summary: Four northeastern Ohio massage parlors suspected of engaging in labor and sex trafficking were raided this morning by law enforcement task forces organized under the office of Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost.

caseTitle: Court Hits Defunct Electronics Recycler with $3 Million Penalty for Hazardous Waste Abandonment
summary: A Franklin County judge has ordered two defunct electronics-recycling companies to pay $3 million in civil penalties for abandoning massive quantities of hazardous waste in Columbus, Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost announced today.

caseTitle: AG Yost Names Ohio’s New Solicitor General
summary: Attorney General Dave Yost today announced his pick for Ohio’s 12th solicitor general, the state’s top attorney for appeals in state and federal courts.

caseTitle: Human Trafficking Investigation Leads to Rape Charges; Public's Help Requested
summary: A Monroe County man accused of raping a child is facing nine felony charges following an investigation by the Southeastern Ohio Human Trafficking Task Force.

caseTitle: Child Predator Sentenced to Prison in Hocking County Rape Case
summary: A 54-year-old Washington County man has been sentenced to 15 years in prison for his part in the rape of two minors, Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost announced today.

caseTitle: At Sixth Annual Human Trafficking Summit, AG Yost Celebrates Survivors, Launches New Training
summary: Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost celebrated survivor “ambassadors” and launched new training for first responders at the office’s sixth annual Human Trafficking Summit today in downtown Columbus.

caseTitle: AG Yost Sues Former Service Dog Charity and Founder for Misuse of More Than $25,000 in Donations
defendants: Former Service Dog Charity and Founder
summary: Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost has filed a lawsuit against a now-defunct Findlay nonprofit and its founder, alleging serious misuse of charitable funds, mismanagement, and abuse of public trust.

caseTitle: AG Yost’s Statement on Fallen Lorain Police Officer
summary: Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost issued the following statement regarding the death of Phillip Wagner, a Lorain police officer, who was killed in the line of duty on Wednesday. Two additional officers were wounded in the apparent targeted attack on law enforcement.

caseTitle: AG Yost Urges the NCAA to Restore Female Athletes’ Records Wrongfully Erased by Male Competitors
summary: Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost and 26 of his counterparts are calling on the NCAA to restore the records, titles, and other honors earned in recent years by female athletes but denied because of policies allowing biological males to participate in female sports.

caseTitle: Yost Vows to Defend School Choice Program
summary: With families preparing for the start of a new academic year, Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost, House Speaker Matt Huffman and other supporters of the state’s EdChoice Scholarship Program held a press conference to clarify the status of the program and reinforce their optimism that it will survive an ongoing court battle despite a recent trial court decision declaring it unconstitutional.

caseTitle: Guilty Plea Filed in Wayne County Child Rape Case
summary: A Wayne County man accused of sexually assaulting six children over nine years has pleaded guilty, Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost announced today.

caseTitle: Former Caregiver at Retirement Community in Ashtabula County Indicted on Rape Charges
summary: A former caregiver at a Conneaut retirement community has been indicted on felony rape charges for alleged sex abuse involving elderly residents, Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost announced today.

caseTitle: 9 Medicaid Providers Indicted on Fraud Charges
summary: In indictments filed this week by the office of Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost, nine Medicaid providers are accused of stealing a combined $1.2 million from the government health-care program for the needy.

caseTitle: Yost Calls for Creation of County Coalition to Spearhead Property-Tax Reform
summary: Attorney General Dave Yost today urged elected county officials to form a coalition to reform property taxes in Ohio before a citizen-led ballot initiative simply eliminates the system altogether.

caseTitle: Title and Summary Certified for Proposed ‘Equal Rights’ Constitutional Amendment
summary: The Ohio Attorney General’s Office today accepted the petition title and summary of a proposed constitutional amendment entitled “Ohio Equal Rights Amendment” that seeks to add a Section 23 to Article I of the Ohio Constitution and to revise Article XV, Section 11.

caseTitle: Consumer Protection: Yost Takes Legal Action in Three ‘Failure to Deliver’ Cases
summary: In separate cases, Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost this week sued three businesses in three counties accused of ripping off consumers.

caseTitle: 13 Medicaid Providers Facing Fraud Charges
summary: In indictments filed this month by the office of Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost, 13 Medicaid providers are accused of defrauding the government health-care program for the needy.

caseTitle: Statement from AG Yost on U.S. Supreme Court Ruling
summary: Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost issued the following statement regarding the U.S. Supreme Court’s ruling in Trump v. CASA:

caseTitle: AG Yost Urges Veto for Stadium Funding Provision
summary: Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost urges a veto to remove language from the pending state budget that would otherwise use taxpayers’ unclaimed funds for the Browns’ Brook Park Stadium Project.

caseTitle: Yost Takes Insurer to Court Over First Responders’ Unpaid Medical Bills
summary: Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost is taking legal action to block an entity from engaging in the business of insurance in Ohio after it failed to obtain a required license and stopped covering medical bills for retired first responders, putting them on the hook for hundreds of thousands of dollars.

caseTitle: Ex-Doctor Sentenced in $1.5 Million Medicaid Scheme
summary: A former Columbus doctor was sentenced this morning to 24 months in prison for fraudulently billing $1.5 million to Medicaid, Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost announced.

caseTitle: AG Yost Addresses Importance of Elder Abuse Awareness and Protection Efforts
summary: With advocates around the globe marking World Elder Abuse Awareness Month, Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost on Monday called on all Ohioans to help raise awareness of elder exploitation and to encourage older relatives and friends to report any victimization they suffer.

caseTitle: Statement from AG Yost on U.S. Supreme Court Ruling
summary: Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost issued the following statement regarding the U.S. Supreme Court’s ruling in United States v. Skrmetti.

caseTitle: AG Yost Approves Conditional Sale of Summa Health
summary: Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost has conditionally approved the sale of Summa Health to General Catalyst subsidiary Health Assurance Transformation LLC (HATCo).

caseTitle: Yost Co-leads Bipartisan Coalition Targeting Gambling Loophole
summary: A bipartisan group of 36 attorneys general co-led by Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost is challenging a federal court ruling that risks creating a loophole for unregulated gambling.

caseTitle: 55 Attorneys General Agree to $7.4 Billion Purdue Settlement; Ohio to Receive Up to $198 Million
summary: Attorney General Dave Yost today announced that the attorneys general of 49 states, the District of Columbia and five U.S. territories have signed on to a $7.4 billion settlement with Purdue Pharma and its owners, the Sackler family.

caseTitle: Changes to Ohio Peace Officer Basic Training to Take Effect in July 2025
summary: Ohio will usher in the most comprehensive overhaul of law enforcement training in decades when training academies next month begin teaching an updated curriculum that prioritizes modern-day policing issues.

caseTitle: Yost Awards Additional $2.3M in Grants Targeting Inmate Addiction in Jails
summary: A second round of grants totaling $2.3 million is headed to jails across the state to help sheriffs prioritize addiction treatment for inmates, Attorney General Dave Yost announced today.

caseTitle: New Task Force Makes First Arrests in Crackdown on Organized Retail Theft
summary: The recent arrests of two suspected fraudsters mark the first success of a new task force created to thwart organized retail theft, Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost, Wood County Sheriff Mark Wasylyshyn and Perrysburg Township Police Chief Matt Gazarek announced today.

caseTitle: AG Yost’s Statement on Insight’s Reopening of Trumbull Regional Medical Center and Hillside Rehabilitation Hospital
summary: Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost met with Insight Health System on Tuesday, June 3 to discuss their plan to reopen Trumbull Regional Medical Center and Hillside Rehabilitation Hospital.

caseTitle: Violent Human Traffickers Sentenced to Life in Prison
summary: Two members of a violent human trafficking ring that operated in south Columbus were sentenced today to life in prison, Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost announced.

caseTitle: AG Yost Sues Concrete Contractor Over Unfinished Work
summary: Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost is suing a central Ohio contractor for allegedly abandoning concrete jobs and engaging in deceptive business practices.

caseTitle: AG Yost Permanently Bars Robocaller Responsible for Billions of Illegal Calls
summary: Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost and his counterparts in seven other states have secured a permanent ban against a Texas-based robocaller behind billions of illegal calls, including millions sent to Ohioans.

caseTitle: Statement on Fallen Morrow County Deputy
summary: Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost issued the following statement regarding the death of Morrow County Deputy Sheriff Daniel Weston Sherrer, who was killed in the line of duty on Memorial Day evening.

caseTitle: Toledo Investment Firm Executive Pleads Guilty in Decade-long Ponzi Scheme
summary: A Toledo investment firm executive who was among multiple co-defendants accused of deceiving and defrauding investors in a decade-long Ponzi scheme pleaded guilty today.

caseTitle: AG Yost Applauds Cleveland Student Finalists in District’s First Year of ‘Do the Write Thing’ Anti-Violence Program
summary: Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost commended 10 Cleveland students for their captivating essays offering ideas for reducing youth violence in their communities.

caseTitle: Yost Leads Brief Defending Law That Targets Threats Against Public Servants
summary: Led by Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost, a bipartisan group of 41 states and the District of Columbia is defending a New Jersey law that protects public servants from increasing threats of violence in the digital age.

caseTitle: Yost Honors Ohio’s Past and Present Victim Advocates at 2025 Two Days in May Conference
summary: Addressing the nearly 1,000 victim advocates attending the office’s annual Two Days in May Conference on Victim Assistance, Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost celebrated the work being done to aid Ohio’s crime victims and echoed the underlying message of this year’s conference theme, “Every Step a Victory.”

caseTitle: AG Yost Releases 2024 Missing Children’s Report
summary: Release Coincides with State, National Events Recognizing Missing Persons Cases

caseTitle: AG Yost Launches Second Series of Virtual Reality Trainings for Law Enforcement
summary: Continuing to uphold his commitment to provide technologically advanced training for law enforcement, Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost is launching a second series of immersive virtual reality courses for Ohio’s 900 law enforcement agencies.

caseTitle: Petition Title and Summary of Proposed Constitutional Amendment to Eliminate Property Taxes in Ohio Is Accepted
summary: The Ohio Attorney General’s Office today accepted the petition title and summary of a proposed constitutional amendment, “Abolishment of Taxes on Real Property,” that seeks to add a Section 14 to Article XII of the Ohio Constitution.

caseTitle: Four Who Died in Line of Duty Remembered at Ohio Peace Officers Memorial Ceremony
summary: In a solemn ceremony today at the Ohio Peace Officer Training Academy, Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost honored the lives of four Ohio peace officers who died in the line of duty in recent years and others who have sacrificed before them.

caseTitle: Two Members of Violent Central Ohio Human Trafficking Ring Convicted
defendants: Two Members of Violent Central Ohio Human Trafficking Ring
summary: Two members of a violent human trafficking ring in south Columbus were convicted today of numerous felonies, including aggravated murder and trafficking in persons, Attorney General Dave Yost and Franklin County Prosecutor Shayla Favor announced.

caseTitle: AG Yost Praises Zanesville Student Finalists in ‘Do the Write Thing’ Anti-Violence Initiative
summary: Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost commended 10 Zanesville middle-school students for their captivating essays offering ideas for reducing youth violence to create a safer community.

caseTitle: Title and Summary Language Certified for Proposed Referendum on Senate Bill 1
summary: The Ohio Attorney General’s Office today certified summary language for a proposed referendum aimed at repealing Senate Bill 1, which authorizes changes to laws governing higher education, including new mandates to eliminate diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) programs.

caseTitle: AG Yost Applauds Canton Students’ Anti-Violence Essays Written for ‘Do the Write Thing’ Program
summary: Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost commended 18 Canton middle-school students for their compelling essays offering ideas for reducing youth violence to create a safer community.

caseTitle: Statement on Fallen Hamilton County Deputy Sheriff
summary: Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost issued the following statement regarding the death of a Hamilton County deputy sheriff, who was killed in the line of duty today.

caseTitle: Former Executive Director of VFW of Ohio Charities Pleads Guilty in Charitable Theft Case
summary: The former leader of a statewide veterans charity pleaded guilty today in connection with the theft of more than $35,000 from a central Ohio VFW post, Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost announced today.

caseTitle: 8 Indicted on Human Trafficking Charges in Trumbull County
summary: An 83-count indictment filed in Trumbull County accuses eight people of running a human trafficking ring that forced women into prostitution at illicit massage parlors in the Mahoning Valley, Attorney General Dave Yost and Mahoning County Sheriff Jerry Greene announced today.

caseTitle: AG Yost Celebrates Springfield Student Finalists in ‘Do the Write Thing’ Anti-Violence Initiative
summary: Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost has partnered with Springfield for five years on the Do the Write Thing Initiative and today his office recognized nine Springfield middle-school students for their moving essays about ways to create safer communities.

caseTitle: Attorney General Yost Sues ShotStop Over Sale of Counterfeit Body Armor Plates
summary: Attorney General Dave Yost is suing a body armor company, its affiliate and the owner of the companies for allegedly selling unsafe equipment to Ohio consumers.

caseTitle: Yost Cracks Down on Shady Used-Car Dealerships
summary: Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost is suing six defunct used-car dealerships that failed to provide vehicle titles to dozens of consumers.

caseTitle: Pickerington Drug Trafficking Case Aligns with Recent Uptick in Carfentanil Seizures
summary: A recent drug trafficking case in Pickerington highlights the ongoing efforts of law enforcement to curb the availability of illicit opioids in the state and the importance of advanced chemical testing to identify emerging drug trends, Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost announced today.

caseTitle: Arrest Made Following Indictment in 2001 Cold Case
summary: A man believed to be responsible for his ex-wife’s disappearance and death has been indicted in Huron County, Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost and Huron County Prosecutor James Sitterly announced.

caseTitle: Identity Restored to Cleveland Man Missing Since 1980
summary: A 20-year-old Cleveland resident who went missing 45 years ago has been identified through advanced DNA technology, the latest in a string of collaborative scientific successes involving unknown remains in Ohio.

caseTitle: AG Yost Celebrates Earth Day by Awarding Grants to Combat Illegal Dumping
summary: In celebration of Earth Day, Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost today began the rollout of nearly $1 million in grants to more than two dozen local governments statewide to clean up existing illegal dump sites containing solid wastes and/or demolition debris and to prevent future dumping.

caseTitle: Summary of Petition to End Qualified Immunity for Police Officers and Other Government Employees Certified
summary: The Ohio Attorney General’s Office today certified the petition summary for a proposed constitutional amendment in compliance with a preliminary injunction entered by the U.S. District Court for the Southern District of Ohio that held Ohio’s nearly century-old ballot initiative process was unconstitutional.

caseTitle: Dayton Woman Gets Prison for $1.5 Million Theft From Medicaid Program
summary: A Montgomery County woman banned previously from working as a Medicaid provider was sentenced this morning to six to nine years in prison for yet again defrauding the program – this time stealing $1.5 million, Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost announced.

caseTitle: Former Nonprofit Director Indicted in Theft of $150K
summary: The former director of a Toledo-area nonprofit has been indicted for allegedly stealing at least $150,000 from the organization and its donors, including elderly Ohioans, Attorney General Dave Yost announced today.

caseTitle: Yost Sues Wholesale Mortgage Lender Over Deceptive Business Practices
summary: Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost is suing United Wholesale Mortgage, accusing the Michigan-based lender of conspiring with brokers to rip off Ohio consumers.

caseTitle: AG Yost Warns Med Spas: Stop Misleading Consumers About Weight-Loss Drugs
summary: Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost has sent letters to 14 medical spas throughout the state warning them to stop making false or misleading claims about their versions of popular weight-loss drugs.

caseTitle: AG Yost Recognizes Youngstown Students for Essays Proposing Solutions to Violence
summary: Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost applauded ten Youngstown middle-school students this afternoon for their award-winning essays about ways to build safer communities to counter the violence they have experienced or witnessed.

caseTitle: BCI Recovers More Than $35,000 in Stolen Bitcoin for Hamilton County Victim
summary: A special unit within the Ohio Bureau of Criminal Investigation recently returned more than $35,000 to a Cincinnati-area woman who fell victim to a cryptocurrency scam, Attorney General Dave Yost and Delhi Township Police Chief Jeff Braun announced today.

caseTitle: Yost Leads Brief Opposing Illinois' Sanctuary Laws
summary: Led by Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost, 23 state attorneys general are supporting the federal government’s legal challenge of sanctuary immigration policies in Illinois.

caseTitle: Yost Seeks to Co-lead Lawsuit Over Drug Company's Cover-up of Cancer Risks
summary: Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost is seeking a lead role in a securities class-action lawsuit accusing the pharmaceutical company GSK, formerly known as GlaxoSmithKline, of concealing the cancer risks of its once-bestselling drug, fueling billions in losses for investors.

caseTitle: Statement on Motion for Stay in Moe v. Yost
defendants: the Ohio Supreme Court in Moe v
summary: Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost issued the following statement regarding a motion for stay filed today with the Ohio Supreme Court in Moe v. Yost.

caseTitle: Yost Seeks to Lead Lawsuit Claiming Target Cost Investors Billions
summary: Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost is asking a federal judge to be named lead plaintiff in a securities class-action lawsuit accusing Target of misleading investors about the financial risks of initiatives promoting the company’s divisive social agenda.

caseTitle: Home-Improvement Scammer Sentenced to Prison
summary: A convicted Indiana felon was sentenced today to 3 to 4½ years in prison for defrauding 14 Ohioans of more than $105,000, Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost announced.

caseTitle: Indictment Filed in Lancaster Double Homicide
summary: A Fairfield County man is facing more than a dozen felony charges, including aggravated murder, in connection with the deaths of two family members and the assault of a third, Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost and Fairfield County Prosecutor Kyle Witt announced.

caseTitle: Report: Still No Sign of Progress in Ohio's Capital-Punishment Stalemate
summary: With the release today of the “2024 Capital Crimes Report,” Attorney General Dave Yost stressed that little, if anything, has changed in the past year to fix Ohio’s broken and costly capital-punishment system.

caseTitle: Eight Counties Awarded First of $60M in Grants Targeting Inmate Addiction in Jails
summary: Grants totaling more than $1.2 million are headed to eight Ohio counties to help sheriffs prioritize addiction treatment for jail inmates, Attorney General Dave Yost announced today.

caseTitle: Cell Phone Repairman Who Stashed Customers' Nude Photos Sentenced to Prison
defendants: Cell Phone Repairman Who Stashed Customers' Nude Photos
summary: A Gallia County man who worked for a cell phone repair business was sentenced to prison today for downloading nude photos from customers’ phones, Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost announced today.

caseTitle: Multistate Human Trafficker Sentenced to Prison
summary: A 32-year-old Cleveland man who abused 12 women across multiple states to force them into prostitution for his own financial gain was sentenced today to 30 to 37½ years in prison, Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost and Cuyahoga County Prosecutor Michael O’Malley announced.

caseTitle: AG Yost Secures $49.1 Million Settlement In Price-Fixing Cases Involving Generic Drugs
summary: Consumers who were charged too much for certain generic drugs may be eligible for compensation from a $49.1 million settlement with two manufacturers, Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost announced today.

caseTitle: Statement on Motion for Stay in Moe v. Yost
defendants: the 10th District Court of Appeals in Moe v
summary: Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost issued the following statement regarding a motion filed today with the 10th District Court of Appeals in Moe v. Yost. The filing seeks to pause the court’s March 18 ruling as the state appeals to the Ohio Supreme Court.

caseTitle: Ohio to Co-lead Class-Action Lawsuit Over Boeing’s Safety Failures
summary: A Delaware judge has appointed two Ohio pension funds as co-lead plaintiffs in a class-action lawsuit seeking accountability from Boeing’s board of directors for a pattern of safety and compliance failures that have harmed the company and its investors.

caseTitle: AG Yost Sues Company for Allegedly Defrauding Homeowners
summary: Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost is suing a Baltimore, Ohio-company and its owner for allegedly defrauding homeowners of nearly $12,000 by providing incomplete or poor-quality remodeling work.

caseTitle: Yost Sues Home Décor Retailer Over $380K in Alleged Consumer Losses
summary: Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost is suing a Youngstown-area retailer specializing in luxury home décor after hundreds of customers reported paying for furnishings that never arrived.

caseTitle: Yost Seeks Lead Role in Investor Lawsuit Against Owner of Cash App, Square
summary: Attorney General Dave Yost is seeking lead-plaintiff status in a securities class-action lawsuit accusing the financial tech company Block Inc. of misleading investors and causing $12.6 million in losses for Ohio’s largest public pension fund, plus billions more for other investors.

caseTitle: Statement on Moe v. Yost Ruling
summary: Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost issued the following statement in response to the 10th District Court of Appeals ruling in Moe v. Yost:

caseTitle: Investigative Partnership Leads to Identity of Stark County John Doe
summary: The skeletal remains of a man found more than 23 years ago in Stark County have finally been identified, thanks to decades of work by investigators and the continual development of DNA technology, Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost and Stark County Sheriff Eric Weisburn announced today.

caseTitle: Paulding County Man Sentenced to Prison for Sexually Abusing a Minor
summary: A Paulding County man who sexually abused a minor was sentenced to 34 months in prison today, Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost and Paulding County Sheriff Jason Landers announced.

caseTitle: Dayton Woman Pleads Guilty to $1.5 Million Theft From Medicaid Program
defendants: Medicaid Program A Montgomery County woman banned previously from working as a Medicaid provider pleaded guilty today
summary: A Montgomery County woman banned previously from working as a Medicaid provider pleaded guilty today for yet again defrauding the program – this time stealing $1.5 million, Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost announced.

caseTitle: Search for Underage Victim of Human Trafficking Leads to Arrest of 2 Suspects
summary: Two Texas men suspected by law enforcement of human trafficking who traveled to central Ohio with four victims, including one juvenile, have been apprehended by the Central Ohio Human Trafficking Task Force, Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost announced today.

caseTitle: Yost Joins Coalition in Defending Parental Rights in Education
summary: Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost and 25 other state attorneys general are urging the U.S. Supreme Court to ensure that parents have the final say in their children’s education and religious upbringing.

caseTitle: Yost, Other AGs Urge Passage of Bill Expanding Access to E15 Gasoline
summary: Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost has joined 15 other state attorneys general in urging Congress to help jump-start American energy production by expanding access to E15 gasoline.

caseTitle: Task Force Arrests Highlight Diverse Backgrounds of Those Fueling Human Trafficking
summary: Two recent arrests by a human trafficking task force underscore the diverse backgrounds of those who attempt to trade money for sex, contributing to the scourge of human trafficking in Ohio.

caseTitle: Yost, Other AGs and DOJ Submit Final Proposed Fix to End Google’s Search Monopoly
summary: Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost and a coalition of 37 state attorneys general and the Justice Department today proposed a final package of remedies to end Google’s illegal monopoly over internet search engines and to restore competition to benefit consumers.

caseTitle: Attorney General Issues 2024 Concealed Carry Report
summary: Attorney General Dave Yost today issued Ohio’s annual report on concealed handgun licensing, showing that a total of 73,552 permits were issued in the state in 2024.
